title: Surely You're Joking, Mr. Feynman!
subtitle: Adventures of a Curious Character
themes: Curiosity-driven learning and the joy of discovery
Rebellion against intellectual authority and convention
Scientific integrity and the dangers of self-deception
Playfulness as a gateway to creative problem-solving
The value of first-principles thinking across domains
excerpt: A riotous collection of anecdotes from Nobel-winning physicist Richard Feynman's life — safecracking at Los Alamos, learning to paint, picking up women in bars, and dismantling authority with gleeful irreverence — that reveals the playful, insatiably curious mind behind the Feynman diagrams.
rationale: Book #2 for scientists-and-mathematicians. Paired with Longitude (Dava Sobel) as a contrasting model of scientific biography: Sobel's is tightly focused on one problem across centuries; Feynman's is a sprawling, first-person picaresque that shatters the stereotype of the stuffy scientist. Chosen for its cultural staying power, its demystification of genius as relentless curiosity, and its value as a primary source in a subcategory dominated by third-person biographies.
